headline: Matriculation Programme 90% Bumiputera Despite Promise of 10% Non-Bumiputera
context: The matriculation programme, which serves as a fast-track to public university, maintains a 90:10 Bumiputera to non-Bumiputera ratio despite pledges in 2019 to increase non-Bumiputera seats.

cards.big: Matriculation offers a 1-year pathway to university versus STPM's 2 years.
cards.sub: 90% of seats are reserved for Bumiputera. Non-Bumiputera students must take the longer, harder route.

cards.big: Matriculation GPA requirements for university admission are lower than STPM.
cards.sub: An STPM student with a 3.5 GPA competes against matriculation students with 3.0 — and loses.

cards.big: The 2019 pledge to increase non-Bumiputera seats from 10% to 25% was reversed.
cards.sub: After political backlash, the ratio returned to 90:10 within 6 months.
STPM costs average RM2,400 per student. Non-Bumiputera families pay more for a harder, longer qualification.

cards.big: Two pathways to the same university. One is shorter, easier, and free. The other is longer, harder, and costs money. Race determines which path you walk.
A single pre-university examination for all students would eliminate the dual-track inequity. Affirmative action can operate through scholarships and support, not separate standards.
